message.content:
Adversarially VERIFY this research claim about how a top deep-research system scores on DeepResearch Bench.

CLAIM: BIGGEST WIN: the actual generated report text for every system on the leaderboard is publicly downloadable, per task ID. I have pulled task 72 for Tavily and for the current #1. You can now read exactly what a 52.44 and a 55.78 report look like, instead of inferring.
DETAIL: The leaderboard space hosts data/raw_data/<system>.jsonl, one JSON per line with keys {id, prompt, article}, id 1-100 matching benchmark task IDs. Task 72 = 'Please write a literature review on the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence (AI) on the labor market...'. I downloaded 11 systems. Saved locally to /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/: tavily.jsonl, cellcog.jsonl (= drb_cellcog_max, the GPT-5.5-judge #1), baidu-qianfan-drs-pro.jsonl (the Gemini-judge #1), deepinsight.jsonl, onyx.jsonl. Base URL pattern for the rest: https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/resolve/main/data/raw_data/<name>.jsonl (names: baidu-qianfan-drs-pro, drb_cellcog_max, xiaoyi_research_agent_0304, deepinsight, onyx, deepsynth, TrajectoryKit, RecallRadar, 1688AILab-DeepResearch-0325, ms_deepresearch, nvidia-aiq-nemotron-gpt52-updated, grep-v4, tavily-research, gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch, openai-deepresearch, claude-research, kimi-researcher, doubao-deepresearch, langchain-open-deep-research[-gpt-5], perplexity-Research, dr-tulu, raaa-deep-research, salesforce-air-deep-research, gensee-search-gpt-5, tongyi-deepresearch-30B-A3B, nvidia-aiq-research-assistant). Caveat: deepinsight.jsonl stores articles with literal backslash-n escapes -- unescape before parsing or your structure metrics will read as zero.
SYSTEM: other
SOURCE GIVEN: https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/tree/main/data/raw_data
STATED CONFIDENCE: high

Go to the source. Check it actually says this. Check it is CURRENT (the benchmark's leaderboard and evaluator both
changed in 2026 — the original paper's numbers are stale; Gemini-2.5-Pro was replaced by GPT-5.5 as the RACE judge).
Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot confirm it from a primary source. We would rather have five verified facts
than thirty plausible ones.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.description: List HF space raw_data dir via API

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus && for f in tavily cellcog baidu-qianfan-drs-pro deepinsight onyx; do echo "=== $f"; python3 -c "
import json,sys
lines=open('$f.jsonl').read().splitlines()
print('lines',len(lines))
o=json.loads(lines[0]); print('keys',list(o.keys()))
ids=[json.loads(l)['id'] for l in lines]
print('id range',min(ids),max(ids),'type',type(ids[0]).__name__)
for l in lines:
    d=json.loads(l)
    if str(d['id'])=='72':
        print('T72 prompt:',d['prompt'][:120].replace(chr(10),' '))
        print('T72 article len:',len(d['article']),'newlines:',d['article'].count(chr(10)),'literal-bslash-n:',d['article'].count(chr(92)+'n'))
"; done; echo "=== CSVs"; cat leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv; echo; cat leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv
input.description: Inspect local jsonl corpus and csvs

input.command: curl -sL "https://huggingface.co/api/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/tree/main/data/raw_data" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(x['path'].split('/')[-1], x['size']) for x in d]"

=== grep for cellcog/tavily naming in create_leaderboard/tabs
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""
Lazy-loaded shared data cache for data viewer tabs.
Loads data_viewer.jsonl once on first access, not at import time.
"""

from __future__ import annotations
import json
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent

# Bucket mount point (HF Storage Bucket mounted at /data in Space runtime)
_BUCKET_DIR = Path("/data")
# Prefer bucket path if available, fallback to repo-local path
DATA_VIEWER_FILE = (
    _BUCKET_DIR / "data_viewer.jsonl"
    if (_BUCKET_DIR / "data_viewer.jsonl").exists()
    else BASE_DIR / "data" / "data_viewer.jsonl"
)
DATA_VIEWER_INDEX_FILE = BASE_DIR / "data" / "data_viewer_index.json"

_REQUIRED_COLS = [
    "model_name", "id", "prompt", "article", "overall_score",
    "comprehensiveness_score", "insight_score",
    "instruction_following_score", "readability_score",
]

_cache: pd.DataFrame | None = None
_index_cache: dict | None = None


def get_data() -> pd.DataFrame:
    global _cache
    if _cache is not None:
        return _cache

    records = []
    if DATA_VIEWER_FILE.exists():
        with DATA_VIEWER_FILE.open(encoding="utf-8") as fh:
            for line in fh:
                line = line.strip()
                if not line:
                    continue
                try:
                    records.append(json.loads(line))
                except json.JSONDecodeError:
                    continue

    df = pd.DataFrame(records)
    if df.empty or not all(c in df.columns for c in _REQUIRED_COLS):
        _cache = pd.DataFrame(columns=_REQUIRED_COLS)
    else:
        df["id"] = df["id"].astype(str)
        _cache = df
    return _cache

content.text:
I have enough to rule. Summary of verification against the primary source (HF Space `muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard`, last modified 2026-06-28).

**Confirmed:**
- `data/raw_data/<name>.jsonl` exists and is downloadable via `.../resolve/main/data/raw_data/<name>.jsonl` — 30 files, all names in the claim's list resolve.
- Each file: 100 lines, keys `{id, prompt, article}`, integer ids 1–100. Task 72 = the AI-labor-market literature review in every file. Confirmed on all 5 local files in `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/`.
- The `deepinsight.jsonl` caveat is real: task-72 article has 0 real newlines, 331 literal `\n` sequences.
- tavily-research = 52.44 overall (Gemini-2.5-Pro judge, `data/leaderboard.csv`, race_result 0.5244). Local `tavily.jsonl` is byte-identical in article length to the viewer copy.
- Local `cellcog.jsonl` md5-matches remote `drb_cellcog_max.jsonl`.

**Falsified:**
- "the report text for **every system on the leaderboard**": the Gemini leaderboard has 45 rows; only 30 have raw_data files. Missing include the entire top 6 (`qianfan_deepresearch_0430` 58.03, `ZTE-Nebula...`, `Link`, `zhipu_deep_research`, `xiaoyi`, `WhaleCloud-DocChain`) and `cellcog-max`, `grep-v5`, `deepdog`, `MindDR-V1.5`, `thinkdepthai`, all sonar/grok rows.
- "cellcog.jsonl (= drb_cellcog_max, the GPT-5.5-judge #1 / 55.78)": **wrong file**. `drb_cellcog_max` and `cellcog-max` are distinct submissions with distinct evaluations — `data/raw_results/drb_cellcog_max/race_result.txt` = 0.5613, `data/raw_results/cellcog-max/race_result.txt` = 0.5667, `data_gpt55/raw_results/cellcog-max/race_result.txt` = 0.5578. No `cellcog-max.jsonl` exists in raw_data. So the downloaded article is *not* the 55.78 report.
- "baidu-qianfan-drs-pro (the Gemini-judge #1)": it scores 0.5422 (54.22) and does not appear on the leaderboard CSV at all. The Gemini #1 is `qianfan_deepresearch_0430` (58.03), whose articles are not published.
- Judge mixing: 52.44 is a Gemini-judge score; 55.78 is a GPT-5.5-judge score. tavily-research isn't on the GPT-5.5 board at all, so "a 52.44 vs a 55.78 report" is a cross-judge comparison.

**Better source found:** `data/data_viewer.jsonl` (199 MB) has `{model_name, id, prompt, article, overall_score, comprehensiveness_score, insight_score, instruction_following_score, readability_score}` — i.e. per-task scores alongside the article (tavily-research task 72 = 50.27 overall). Same 30-model coverage.

input.reasoning:
The mechanism is real and current, but the load-bearing identifications are wrong.

CONFIRMED against the primary source (HF Space muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard, lastModified 2026-06-28):
- data/raw_data/<name>.jsonl exists, is downloadable at https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/resolve/main/data/raw_data/<name>.jsonl, and every name in the claim's list resolves (30 files total).
- Each file is 100 lines with keys {id, prompt, article}, integer ids 1-100; task 72 is the AI/labor-market literature review in all files checked (tavily, cellcog, baidu-qianfan-drs-pro, deepinsight, onyx in /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/).
- The deepinsight caveat is true: its task-72 article has 0 real newlines and 331 literal backslash-n sequences.
- tavily-research = 52.44 overall under the Gemini-2.5-Pro judge (data/leaderboard.csv; data/raw_results/tavily-research/race_result.txt = 0.5244).
- Local cellcog.jsonl is md5-identical to remote drb_cellcog_max.jsonl (c02f27b3217960104447e91388948609).

FALSIFIED:
1. "every system on the leaderboard" -- the Gemini-judge leaderboard has 45 rows, raw_data has only 30 files. Missing: the entire top 6 (qianfan_deepresearch_0430 58.03, ZTE-Nebula-DeepResearch-V20260519, Link, zhipu_deep_research, xiaoyi, WhaleCloud-DocChain), plus cellcog-max, grep-v5 (only grep-v4 published), 1688AILab-0428 (only 0325), deepdog, MindDR-V1.5, thinkdepthai, and all sonar/grok/gpt-4o rows.
2. "cellcog.jsonl (= drb_cellcog_max, the GPT-5.5-judge #1)" is the WRONG FILE. drb_cellcog_max and cellcog-max are distinct submissions with distinct evaluations: data/raw_results/drb_cellcog_max/race_result.txt = Overall 0.5613; data/raw_results/cellcog-max/race_result.txt = 0.5667; data_gpt55/raw_results/cellcog-max/race_result.txt = 0.5578 (the 55.78). There is no cellcog-max.jsonl in raw_data. So the downloaded article is not the 55.78 report -- it is a different, unlisted submission whose GPT-5.5 score does not exist.
3. "baidu-qianfan-drs-pro (the Gemini-judge #1)" is wrong: its race_result.txt = 0.5422 and it does not appear on data/leaderboard.csv at all. The Gemini-judge #1 is qianfan_deepresearch_0430 (58.03), whose articles are NOT published in raw_data.
4. Judge mixing: 52.44 is a Gemini-2.5-Pro-judge score; 55.78 is a GPT-5.5-judge score (data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv). tavily-research does not appear on the GPT-5.5 board at all, so "what a 52.44 and a 55.78 report look like" compares scores produced by two different judges on two different leaderboards.

Also unverified: "I downloaded 11 systems" -- only 5 jsonl files exist in /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/.
input.corrected: Corrected statement: The HF Space muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard publishes full report text (id/prompt/article, ids 1-100) for 30 of the ~45 leaderboard entries at https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/resolve/main/data/raw_data/<name>.jsonl -- but NOT for either current #1. GPT-5.5-judge #1 is cellcog-max (55.78, data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv); the published drb_cellcog_max.jsonl is a DIFFERENT submission (Gemini-judge Overall 0.5613 per data/raw_results/drb_cellcog_max/race_result.txt, vs cellcog-max 0.5667). Gemini-judge #1 is qianfan_deepresearch_0430 (58.03, data/leaderboard.csv); baidu-qianfan-drs-pro is a separate, unranked submission scoring 0.5422 (data/raw_results/baidu-qianfan-drs-pro/race_result.txt). Highest-scoring systems with downloadable articles: nvidia-aiq-nemotron-gpt52-updated (55.95), 1688AILab-DeepResearch-0325 (55.39), drb_cellcog (55.31), deepinsight (55.24). Better source than raw_data: data/data_viewer.jsonl (199 MB) carries {model_name, id, prompt, article, overall_score, comprehensiveness_score, insight_score, instruction_following_score, readability_score}, i.e. per-task RACE scores next to each article (e.g. tavily-research task 72 = 50.27 overall), for the same 30 models. Per-task score files also exist at data/raw_results/<name>/raw_results.jsonl and data/raw_results/<name>/race_result.txt.
